verses.arabicText: أَفَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى كَفَرَ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا وَقَالَ لَأُوتَيَنَّ مَالًۭا وَوَلَدًا
verses.translationText: Then, have you seen he who disbelieved in Our verses and said, "I will surely be given wealth and children [in the next life]?"

verses.arabicText: أَطَّلَعَ ٱلْغَيْبَ أَمِ ٱتَّخَذَ عِندَ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ عَهْدًۭا
verses.translationText: Has he looked into the unseen, or has he taken from the Most Merciful a promise?

verses.arabicText: كَلَّا ۚ سَنَكْتُبُ مَا يَقُولُ وَنَمُدُّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ مَدًّۭا
verses.translationText: No! We will record what he says and extend for him from the punishment extensively.
And We will inherit him [in] what he mentions, and he will come to Us alone.

verses.arabicText: وَٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ءَالِهَةًۭ لِّيَكُونُوا۟ لَهُمْ عِزًّۭا
verses.translationText: And they have taken besides Allah [false] deities that they would be for them [a source of] honor.

verses.arabicText: كَلَّا ۚ سَيَكْفُرُونَ بِعِبَادَتِهِمْ وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضِدًّا
verses.translationText: No! Those "gods" will deny their worship of them and will be against them opponents [on the Day of Judgement].

verses.arabicText: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّآ أَرْسَلْنَا ٱلشَّيَٰطِينَ عَلَى ٱلْكَٰفِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّۭا
verses.translationText: Do you not see that We have sent the devils upon the disbelievers, inciting them to [evil] with [constant] incitement?

verses.arabicText: فَلَا تَعْجَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ إِنَّمَا نَعُدُّ لَهُمْ عَدًّۭا
verses.translationText: So be not impatient over them. We only count out to them a [limited] number.

verses.arabicText: يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ وَفْدًۭا
verses.translationText: On the Day We will gather the righteous to the Most Merciful as a delegation
And will drive the criminals to Hell in thirst

verses.arabicText: لَّا يَمْلِكُونَ ٱلشَّفَٰعَةَ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ عِندَ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ عَهْدًۭا
verses.translationText: None will have [power of] intercession except he who had taken from the Most Merciful a covenant.
And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken [for Himself] a son."
You have done an atrocious thing.

verses.arabicText: تَكَادُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ وَتَنشَقُّ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَتَخِرُّ ٱلْجِبَالُ هَدًّا
verses.translationText: The heavens almost rupture therefrom and the earth splits open and the mountains collapse in devastation
That they attribute to the Most Merciful a son.

verses.arabicText: وَمَا يَنۢبَغِى لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا
verses.translationText: And it is not appropriate for the Most Merciful that He should take a son.

verses.arabicText: إِن كُلُّ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّآ ءَاتِى ٱلرَّحْمَٰنِ عَبْدًۭا
verses.translationText: There is no one in the heavens and earth but that he comes to the Most Merciful as a servant.
He has enumerated them and counted them a [full] counting.
And all of them are coming to Him on the Day of Resurrection alone.
